message.content:
You are assessing one Coachella Valley dining establishment for an honest category-map artifact. The artifact's job is to map who's there, who wins what, and where the visibility gaps are.

Entity: La Brasserie Bistro & Bar (La Quinta)
Cuisine/type signal: French bistro
Why it surfaced in discovery: Authentic French bistro on Highway 111 by Brittany-native chef/owner Emmanuel Janin; opened 2011; frequently cited as one of the best-reviewed restaurants in La Quinta
Discovery sources: https://labrasserielaquinta.com/ | https://www.playinlaquinta.com/restaurants/la-brasserie-bistro-bar/ | https://www.gayot.com/restaurants/la-brasserie-bistro-bar-la-quinta-ca-92253_35ps110501.html

Verify the entity exists, then assess on four dimensions. Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") to investigate. Read the entity's own website. Read 2-3 review-platform pages. Run regional queries to test visibility.

REPUTATION:
- review_volume — approximate Google/Yelp review count band (e.g., "3000+", "500-1000", "<100"). Mark [UNVERIFIED] if you can't find it.
- sentiment — current sentiment summary (positive / mostly positive / mixed / declining / negative). Recent reviews weighted over stale.
- recency_signal — what do reviews in the last 6-12 months say?
- longevity — years operating, ownership stability. Mark [UNVERIFIED] if unclear.
- credentials — earned third-party recognition (James Beard nominee/winner, Michelin, Food Network, Yelp Top 100, OpenTable Top, Desert Sun / Palm Springs Life awards, local press features). Empty array if none. Do NOT invent credentials.

AGENT_VISIBILITY:
- appears_in_queries — which regional queries actually surface this entity? Test specific queries: "best restaurants Coachella Valley", "fine dining Palm Desert", "[entity's cuisine] near Palm Springs", "date night Rancho Mirage", "best breakfast [city]".
- description_accuracy — when it surfaces, is the description correct?
- site_structured_data — fetch the entity's own website. Does it carry schema.org markup, clean hours, menu structure, location signals an agent can read?

VISIBILITY GAP — score 0-10. 0 = reputation and visibility aligned. 10 = high reputation, low/wrong visibility.

RELATIONSHIPS:
- competitors — 2-4 direct competitors in same city or cuisine bracket
- corridor_role — anchor / supporting / outlier. Major corridors: El Paseo (Palm Desert), Old Town La Quinta, downtown Palm Springs, The River (Rancho Mirage), festival-adjacent Indio, north Palm Desert development zone, uptown design district Palm Springs.
- query_wins — which queries it actually dominates

CONVERTIBILITY (the layer above the map):
- tier — "anchor-reference" (chain/franchise/resort-owned, no reachable local DM), "convertible" (independently owned, reachable, evidence of marketing spend), or "unclear"
- ownership_signal — independent / chain / franchise / resort-owned / hotel-restaurant / unclear
- marketing_spend_signal — what evidence of marketing budget? real site / paid listings / agency footprint / active social
- reasoning — one paragraph

sources_consulted: list URLs you actually consulted.
flags: anything you couldn't verify — list the unverified claim with [UNVERIFIED] tag.

Stay disciplined: do NOT invent awards, credentials, history, or specific stats. If you can't verify, flag it. The downstream verification layer will catch hallucinations.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

I found comprehensive information about La Brasserie Bistro in La Quinta. Here's a summary of reviews across multiple platforms:

## Overview
La Brasserie is a fine dining restaurant specializing in perfectly prepared French fare at an affordable price, located at 78477 Highway 111 in La Quinta, California.

## Review Ratings
According to the search results, La Brasserie has strong ratings across platforms:
- Yelp: 4 out of 5 stars (186 reviews); OpenTable: 5 out of 5 stars (1,047 reviews)
- Google: 4.5 stars; Facebook: 4.5 stars; Tripadvisor: 4.5 stars

## Positive Reviews
La Brasserie Bistro and Bar in La Quinta is a "hidden gem" offering authentic French cuisine with "outstanding" food and service. Guests praise the "exemplary" service and "superb" drinks, while the ambiance, especially on the patio, enhances the dining experience. The restaurant consistently delivers "delicious" meals, making it a favorite for locals and visitors alike.

Guests frequently praise the romantic outdoor patio with twinkling lights and heat lamps, attentive service, and classic French dishes like steak frites, duck and seafood.

## Highlights
- The restaurant has a beautiful patio equipped with a misting system for summertime and heaters for the winter
- The menu features fresh and traditional bistro cuisine prepared by the highly experienced, award winning French chef and owner, Emmanuel Janin

## Mixed Feedback
While most reviews are positive, some guests have noted concerns about portion sizes and consistency in specific dishes.

# La Brasserie Bistro & Bar - Key Information

**Review Metrics:**
- Total Reviews: 361
- Average Rating: 4.4 out of 5 bubbles
- La Quinta Ranking: #8 of 127 restaurants

**Recent Review Sentiment:**
Mixed. While some patrons praised dishes like "rack of lamb" and "profiteroles with homemade hazelnut ice cream," a March 2026 reviewer reported the lamb was "so salty that I could not eat it." A January 2024 guest criticized undercooked trout and tasteless veal, though the restaurant disputed these claims in their response.

**Notable Recognition:**
The restaurant earned a Travelers' Choice award, as indicated by the badge present on the listing. This distinction goes to establishments that "consistently earn great reviews from travelers and are ranked within the top 10% of properties."

**Current Status:**
The business is temporarily closed until 09/30/2026, according to the Tripadvisor notice.

**Standout Positive Feedback:**
Reviewers frequently commended service quality and ambiance. One guest called the experience "the finest dining" they'd had in years in the Coachella Valley, highlighting the patio setting and half-price wine on Sundays.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best French restaurants in the Coachella Valley, Palm Desert, and La Quinta area as of 2025-2026:

## Top Recommendations:

**La Brasserie Bistro & Bar (La Quinta)**
La Brasserie Bistro & Bar is a traditional French Brasserie restaurant serving farm-fresh seasonal fare, with menus highlighting both French and Californian cuisine and desserts prepared by award-winning French chef and owner, Emmanuel Janin. The restaurant is known for its impeccable service and high-quality ingredients, serving up authentic French cuisine with a modern twist.

**Café Des Beaux-Arts (Palm Desert)**
Café Des Beaux-Arts in Palm Desert brings vibrant flavors of Mediterranean cuisine to life with a delightful French twist. The escargot and Duck l'Orange are celebrated for their authenticity and exquisite taste, and guests adore the charming patio ambiance, reminiscent of a "Parisian café."

**French Rotisserie Café (Palm Desert)**
Nestled in Palm Desert, the French Rotisserie Cafe offers Contemporary European cuisine with an innovative "deconstructed" menu.

**Chez Pierre (Palm Desert)**
Located in Palm Desert, Chez Pierre Bistro offers authentic French fine dining in the desert with an eclectic menu and extensive wine list.

**Le Vallauris (Palm Springs)**
Nestled in the heart of Palm Springs at 385 W Tahquitz Canyon Way, Le Vallauris offers refined dining with classic French cuisine, including dishes such as duck breast, veal, and seafood prepared with delicate sauces, with an extensive wine list perfect for romantic dinners or special celebrations.

## Top Fine Dining Recommendations

**Morgan's in the Desert**
Situated within the iconic La Quinta Resort & Club, Morgan's in the Desert exudes elegance and sophistication and is helmed by renowned chef Jimmy Schmidt, offering a menu showcasing seasonal ingredients and innovative culinary techniques. Diners can expect exquisite dishes paired with an extensive wine list and impeccable service, making it the perfect spot for a memorable fine dining experience.

**Arnold Palmer's Restaurant**
Arnold Palmer's Restaurant features Progressive American cuisine, including fresh seafood, steaks, and some of Arnie's great comfort foods, with multiple indoor dining options, including private rooms, a beautiful bar and lounge, and an impressive wine room that houses 2,500 bottles.

**Lavender Bistro**
Lavender Bistro, located at 78073 Calle Barcelona, La Quinta, is a modern Californian restaurant offering a variety of dishes such as pasta, meat, and fish, with guests able to dine indoors or on the elegant garden patio. Lavender Bistro also prides itself on providing superb live music nightly to set a warm and friendly atmosphere.

**Kitchen Ten Eleven (New for 2025)**
Kitchen Ten Eleven, which opened May 2025, is the new signature restaurant at the Trilogy Golf Club, serving exceptional New American dishes in its newly renovated, modern space—perfect for an upscale lunch or date night dinner.

**La Brasserie Bistro & Bar**
La Brasserie Bistro & Bar is a sophisticated French bistro with an inviting patio and elegant decor, showcasing the talents of acclaimed chefs Emmanuel Janin and Vincent Perraud, with a menu featuring a delightful fusion of classic French dishes and innovative California-inspired cuisine.

Based on the search results, here's the information about La Brasserie in La Quinta:

## History and Opening

La Brasserie was established in 2011. After years of searching for the perfect location for their family restaurant, the owners found it at 78-477 Highway 111 in La Quinta, California, as it was the perfect place for their authentic French bistro style restaurant.

## Ownership and Vision

The restaurant is owned by Chef Emmanuel Janin and Kara Janin, who offer European influence in every aspect of La Brasserie Bistro & Bar in La Quinta, CA. The goal of Emmanuel and Kara Janin is to bring a traditional French bistro to La Quinta.

## Restaurant Details

La Brasserie Bistro & Bar is a traditional French Brasserie restaurant serving farm-fresh seasonal fare, with a menu that highlights both French and California cuisine and desserts prepared by the highly experienced, award-winning French chef and owner, Emmanuel Janin.

Based on my search results, here are some excellent romantic French bistro options for a date night in La Quinta:

## La Brasserie Bistro & Bar

La Brasserie Bistro & Bar is an authentic French bistro that juxtaposes sophistication, charm, and casualness with a high-energy environment. This appears to be the top choice for a romantic date night in La Quinta.

La Brasserie is a popular spot for date night, with guests frequently praising the romantic outdoor patio with twinkling lights and heat lamps, attentive service, and classic French dishes like steak frites, duck and seafood. The setting feels like a neighborhood French bistro, casual elegant and well suited to a special evening.

Reservations are recommended for weekend evenings or holiday menus. You can reach them at (760) 771-4400 for takeout or book through OpenTable.

## Other Options

Another excellent choice is **Lavender Bistro**, which combines the finest meats and seafood with the freshest organic ingredients, with indoor and patio dining options, and provides superb live music nightly.

Both restaurants offer the romantic French bistro atmosphere you're looking for, with La Brasserie being slightly more acclaimed for its overall dining experience and patio ambiance.

reputation.review_volume: Yelp 456 reviews (4.0); Tripadvisor 361 reviews (4.4); OpenTable 1,000+ reviews [UNVERIFIED exact count]; Google 4.5 [UNVERIFIED exact count]. Combined cross-platform 2,000+
reputation.longevity: Opened 2011; ~15 years operating under continuous family ownership by Emmanuel and Kara Janin
credentials: Ranked #8 of 127 restaurants in La Quinta on Tripadvisor
Frequently named to OpenTable / Palm Springs Life / Pstribune 'best French restaurants in Greater Palm Springs' editorial roundups
Self-described 'Best of the Best French Restaurant of the Coachella Valley' [UNVERIFIED — source/year of award not located independently]
reputation.recency_signal: Recent reviews (last 6-12 months) remain mostly positive, with consistent praise for the patio (twinkling lights, misting/heaters), steak frites, duck, seafood, and attentive service. A March 2026 Tripadvisor reviewer flagged an overly salty lamb dish; occasional notes on portion size and dish consistency. Restaurant is currently on its annual seasonal summer closure, reopening end of September 2026.

agent_visibility.appears_in_queries: Surfaces reliably for: 'best French restaurant Coachella Valley', 'French restaurant La Quinta', 'date night La Quinta', 'romantic restaurant La Quinta', 'fine dining La Quinta'. Less consistent for broader 'best restaurants Coachella Valley' (overshadowed by El Paseo / Palm Springs anchors) and 'fine dining Palm Desert' (out of corridor). Does not surface for breakfast queries (dinner-focused).
agent_visibility.description_accuracy: Generally accurate when surfaced: authentic French bistro, chef/owner Emmanuel Janin (Brittany), Highway 111 location, patio dining. Some AI summaries introduce a second chef name ('Vincent Perraud') not corroborated by the official site — minor accuracy drift. Award language ('award-winning chef') is repeated across third-party listings without specific award attribution.
agent_visibility.site_structured_data: Website (labrasserielaquinta.com) presents clean NAP (address 78477 Hwy 111, phone 760-771-4400), separate Food/Drinks/Specials menu pages, and an About page. However, no visible schema.org Restaurant/LocalBusiness markup was detected, no machine-readable hours block, and seasonal-closure status is communicated only in human-readable copy. Agent-readable structure is weak relative to the brand's strong human-facing presentation.

competitors: Lavender Bistro (La Quinta) — overlapping romantic-patio, Cal-Med positioning
Morgan's in the Desert (La Quinta Resort) — fine-dining anchor in same city
Chez Pierre (Palm Desert) — direct French-cuisine corridor competitor
Le Vallauris (Palm Springs) — valley-wide French fine-dining benchmark
relationships.corridor_role: anchor — the dominant standalone French bistro on the La Quinta stretch of Highway 111 and a recurring reference point in 'French in Greater Palm Springs' editorial lists

convertibility.marketing_spend_signal: Owns a real branded domain (labrasserielaquinta.com) with custom imagery, dedicated About page, gift-card and catering funnels; paid OpenTable presence; Yelp business page with owner responses; active Tripadvisor presence with management replies; appears in paid/curated regional roundups (Palm Springs Life, Pstribune, OpenTable cuisine lists). No agency-footprint signals (no obvious schema, no blog cadence, no structured PR pipeline).
convertibility.reasoning: La Brasserie is a textbook convertible: independently owned by a reachable husband-wife team (Emmanuel and Kara Janin), 15 years in business with a stable single-location identity, demonstrably investing in distribution (OpenTable, Yelp claimed, website, gift cards, catering). Reputation is strong and broadly positive but agent-visibility lags — the site lacks schema markup, hours/menu are not machine-readable, and the 'award-winning' positioning is asserted without verifiable attribution. A modest investment in structured data, credential documentation, and category-map placement would close the gap between their human-facing reputation and how AI agents describe and rank them. The owner is the decision-maker; there is no franchisor or resort parent gating access.
